title: Getting started

Lastly, can render result using writer define. ggsql understands multiple backends, vignette focus DuckDB reader since powerful doesn’t require additional setup. create DuckDB reader using aptly named duckdb_reader() function: default creates connection -memory database, can point database file connect instead: reader mainly service executing queries , can something else well, e.g. register data available queries equal footing “real” tables database: can see tables currently available : wanted fetch data back using ggsql_table() (works registered native tables views), unregister using ggsql_unregister() (registered tables) mentioned, main purpose reader backend ggsql query executed. possible execute pure SQL queries get back data.frame: main motivation course create visualizations. vignette go detail ggsql syntax. , head documentation website. familiar ggplot2 able understand gist . can see, executing visual query produce plot, creates data structure contains information necessary render . analogue ggplot object create using ggplot2. can use object introspection, e.g. get visual part query: Finally arrive along: get plot . ggsql works concept writers somewhat analogous graphics devices R. responsible figuring visual representation abstract encoding plot specification. moment ggsql comes vegalite writer also one available R package: Often don’t think since printing result query automatically creates writer renders behaves way ggplot object (also true Quarto/Rmarkdown documents): However, interested capturing writer produces can call ggsql_render() explicitly: might also interested capturing plot static image. can use ggsql_save() function can save output either vegalite json object (like one ), SVG PNG. latter two plot rendered using V8 package final image captured.

ddb <- duckdb_reader() ddb2 <- duckdb_reader("my/amazing/data.db") ggsql_register(ddb, gapminder::gapminder, "gapminder") ggsql_table_names(ddb) #> [1] "gapminder" ggsql_execute_sql(ddb, " SELECT MAX(pop) AS population, country FROM gapminder GROUP BY country ORDER BY population DESC LIMIT 6 ") #>   population       country #> 1 1318683096         China #> 2 1110396331         India #> 3  301139947 United States #> 4  223547000     Indonesia #> 5  190010647        Brazil #> 6  169270617      Pakistan plot <- ggsql_execute(ddb, " SELECT * FROM gapminder WHERE year = 2007  VISUALIZE gdpPercap AS x, lifeExp AS y, continent AS stroke, pop AS size DRAW point   SETTING fill => null SCALE size TO (2, 15) ")  str(plot) #> <ggsql_spec> #> • Rows: 142 #> • Columns: linewidth, pos1, stroke, opacity, shape, pos2, size, fill #> • Layers: 1 ggsql_visual(plot) #> [1] "VISUALIZE gdpPercap AS x, lifeExp AS y, continent AS stroke, pop AS size

title: Getting started
text: ggsql understands multiple backends, vignette focus DuckDB reader since powerful doesn’t require additional setup. create DuckDB reader using aptly named duckdb_reader() function: default creates connection -memory database, can point database file connect instead: reader mainly service executing queries , can something else well, e.g. register data available queries equal footing “real” tables database: can see tables currently available : wanted fetch data back using ggsql_table() (works registered native tables views), unregister using ggsql_unregister() (registered tables)
code: ddb <- duckdb_reader() ddb2 <- duckdb_reader("my/amazing/data.db") ggsql_register(ddb, gapminder::gapminder, "gapminder") ggsql_table_names(ddb) #> [1] "gapminder"

title: Getting started
text: mentioned, main purpose reader backend ggsql query executed. possible execute pure SQL queries get back data.frame: main motivation course create visualizations. vignette go detail ggsql syntax. , head documentation website. familiar ggplot2 able understand gist . can see, executing visual query produce plot, creates data structure contains information necessary render . analogue ggplot object create using ggplot2. can use object introspection, e.g. get visual part query:
code:
ggsql_execute_sql(ddb, " SELECT MAX(pop) AS population, country FROM gapminder GROUP BY country ORDER BY population DESC LIMIT 6 ") #>   population       country #> 1 1318683096         China #> 2 1110396331         India #> 3  301139947 United States #> 4  223547000     Indonesia #> 5  190010647        Brazil #> 6  169270617      Pakistan plot <- ggsql_execute(ddb, " SELECT * FROM gapminder WHERE year = 2007  VISUALIZE gdpPercap AS x, lifeExp AS y, continent AS stroke, pop AS size DRAW point   SETTING fill => null SCALE size TO (2, 15) ")  str(plot) #> <ggsql_spec> #> • Rows: 142 #> • Columns: linewidth, pos1, stroke, opacity, shape, pos2, size, fill #> • Layers: 1 ggsql_visual(plot) #> [1] "VISUALIZE gdpPercap AS x, lifeExp AS y, continent AS stroke, pop AS size
DRAW point
  SETTING fill => null
SCALE size TO (2, 15)"

title: Getting started

title: A SQL extension for declarative data visualization based on the Grammar of Graphics
text: ggsql R package provides rust bindings ggsql visualization tool can hook readers writers , execute queries, visualize result. also contain knitr engine supporting ggsql blocks, facilities bidirectional data flow R, Python, ggsql blocks. means can prepare data one block using dplyr pandas, visualize ggsql different block without anything pass data around.
